title: Safeguard Verification: Isobutane Pump Reinstated (Zero Energy Verification)
objectives: Define what constitutes a safeguard and the requirements for verification.
Recognize when it is necessary to physically verify safeguards.
Demonstrate awareness and appropriate utilization of start work checks to align expectations and estimate time needed to verify safeguards before commencing work.
Develop strategies and use techniques to improve communication skills and facilitate better communication from others throughout planning, preparation, and execution.
Facilitate concise pre-job briefings with 2-way dialog so all team members are informed about job scope, potential hazards, and key steps for successful execution.

sceneResources.title: Permit to Work (PTW)
sceneResources.description: Active work permit for P-101 pump recommissioning

sceneResources.title: Start Work Check (SWC) Form
sceneResources.description: Mandatory checklist for verifying safety requirements before starting work

sceneResources.title: Isolation List - P-101
sceneResources.description: Complete list of all isolation points, locks, and tags for P-101 pump

learner_prompt: You are the outside operator and permit approver-verifier. In each scene, state what you do and say, and why. Ask for missing information. Reference safeguards and procedures explicitly. Focus on verifying safeguards, confirming zero energy (including vent/bleeder verification), and using clear, closed-loop communication.

roleplay_nodes.title: Remote Approval Request
roleplay_nodes.description: Control room requests PTW sign-off so the crew can begin work on isobutane pump recommissioning. Learner must resist premature approval and establish verification expectations.

role_play_persona.name: Mike
role_play_persona.personality: Direct, concise, slightly blunt/sarcastic; prefers structured updates
role_play_persona.objective: Get a clear decision and understand what verification steps will occur before start.
initial_messages: Mike in the control room. They’re asking for your permit sign-off so they can start the pump recommissioning. Can you approve it now, or are you holding it for verification?

tasks.description: Initiate a walkdown with the crew lead/maintenance lead to verify the isolations and safeguards.
tasks.completion_criteria: Learner initiates a walkdown to physically verify the isolations and safeguards.

tasks.description: Learner approves/signs off on PTW immediately without additional verification.
tasks.completion_criteria: Learner indicates they will sign/approve the PTW immediately or says 'approved' before verification steps.

roleplay_nodes.title: Premature Permit Signoff Redirect
roleplay_nodes.description: Redirect the learner to the premature permit signoff redirect.

tasks.description: Redirect the learner to the premature permit signoff redirect.
tasks.completion_criteria: Learner is redirected to the premature permit signoff redirect.

roleplay_nodes.title: Perform Walkdown
roleplay_nodes.description: Request a walkdown with the crew lead/maintenance lead to verify the isolations and safeguards.

role_play_persona.name: Tom
role_play_persona.personality: Practical, field-focused, confident; may be slightly defensive
role_play_persona.roleplay_objective: Represent isolations as implemented; support verification; keep the job moving safely.
initial_messages: Tom with maintenance. We installed the isolations per the plan. Are we ready to start?

tasks.completion_criteria: Learner verifies the isolations and safeguards.

tasks.description: Learner verifies the isolations and safeguards without the isolation list.
tasks.completion_criteria: Learner verifies the isolations and safeguards without the isolation list.

roleplay_nodes.title: Walkthrough and SWC Dialogue
roleplay_nodes.description: Walk through the isolations and safeguards and discuss the start work check.

role_play_persona.name: Tom
role_play_persona.personality: Practical, concise; supports walk-through when prompted
role_play_persona.roleplay_objective: Walk through the isolations and safeguards and discuss the start work check.
initial_messages: Ok, I'll walk through the isolations and safeguards so you can physically verify them.
We've got Breaker VE03A OFF—LOTO lock on, tag on with name/date/permit.

Walking the hard isolations first: inlet from V400 closed—locked/tagged. Pump discharge closed—locked/tagged. Outlet to caustic drum closed—locked/tagged. Vent closed—locked/tagged. Bypass closed—locked/tagged. Return to V400 closed—locked/tagged.

tasks.completion_criteria: Learner asks for zero energy verification.

roleplay_nodes.title: Zero Energy Verification (Linked to SWC)
roleplay_nodes.description: Explicit zero-energy verification section linked to SWC. Most steps appear complete, but the bleeder/vent was never opened. Crew rationalizations may include 'gauge shows zero' or 'control room said zero.' Learner must decide whether to stop or sign off.

role_play_persona.name: Tom
role_play_persona.personality: Confident, practical; may lean on 'normal practice' shortcuts unless challenged
role_play_persona.objective: Get to start work while complying with verification expectations.
initial_messages: We’re ready for zero-energy verification. Ask what you need.
Crew note: gauge is reading zero. Control room said it’s at zero too.

tasks.description: Ask the crew to show exactly how they proved zero energy (not just gauge reading or control room statement). Require closed-loop confirmation.
tasks.completion_criteria: Learner asks 'show me how you verified/proved zero energy' and challenges reliance on gauge/control-room-only confirmation.

tasks.description: Catch that bleeder/vent was not opened; verify zero energy before proceeding.
tasks.completion_criteria: Learner verifies zero energy before proceeding.

tasks.description: Learner approves/signs off on start work without requiring the bleeder/vent to be opened/checked as part of proving zero energy.
tasks.completion_criteria: Learner approves/signs off on start work without requiring the bleeder/vent to be opened/checked as part of proving zero energy.

roleplay_nodes.title: Outcome: Safe Proceed (Bleeder/Vent Caught and Corrected)
roleplay_nodes.description: Proceed with work only after zero energy is proven (including bleeder/vent verification) and start work expectations are aligned.

tasks.description: Proceed with work only after zero energy is proven (including bleeder/vent verification) and start work expectations are aligned.
tasks.completion_criteria: Learner states conditions met and authorizes proceed with closed-loop confirmation.

roleplay_nodes.title: Outcome: Incident (Gap Missed, Flange Break)
roleplay_nodes.description: Work proceeds to flange break. Residual pressure/gas releases; local monitors alarm; workers evacuate. One crew member becomes ill and is transported offsite. Emergency response protocol initiated; no fatality.

role_play_persona.name: Tom
role_play_persona.objective: Get the crew safe and coordinate immediate actions with the verifier and control room.
initial_messages: Stop—stop! We’ve got a release! Back out—get upwind!
One of the guys is dizzy—getting him to fresh air. We need medical response now.

tasks.description: Confirm evacuation/accountability and initiate immediate controls aligned to site protocol (high level; not a quiz).
tasks.completion_criteria: Learner confirms evacuation/accountability, communicates immediate controls/coordination needs, and uses closed-loop communication.

roleplay_nodes.title: Outcome: Incident (Valve Blowby)
roleplay_nodes.description: Work proceeds to valve blowby. Residual pressure/gas releases; local monitors alarm; workers evacuate. One crew member becomes ill and is transported offsite. Emergency response protocol initiated; no fatality.

tasks.description: Valve blowby. Residual pressure/gas releases; local monitors alarm; workers evacuate.
tasks.completion_criteria: Learner confirms valve blowby, communicates immediate controls/coordination needs.

roleplay_nodes.title: Debrief (Safe Outcome) — Lessons Learned
roleplay_nodes.description: Coach guides reflective debrief focusing on what went well: verification discipline, critical safeguard focus, and communication. Explicitly use 'debrief' and 'lessons learned' language.

role_play_persona.name: Coach
role_play_persona.objective: Help learner articulate lessons learned from a successful verification and safe outcome.
initial_messages: Debrief. You prevented an incident by requiring proof of zero energy—not just a gauge reading.
Lessons learned: what did you do well, and how will you apply this verification approach on the next job?

tasks.description: Learner articulates lessons learned (verification discipline, critical safeguards, closed-loop communication).
tasks.completion_criteria: Learner states at least 2 concrete lessons learned tied to safeguards verification and communication behaviors.

roleplay_nodes.title: Debrief (Incident Outcome) — Lessons Learned
roleplay_nodes.description: Coach guides reflective debrief emphasizing the impact of not verifying zero energy (bleeder/vent not opened) and how safeguard verification prevents harm. High-level summary of response; not a detailed emergency response quiz.

role_play_persona.name: Coach
role_play_persona.personality: Reflective, guiding, direct about cause-and-effect without shaming
role_play_persona.objective: Help learner connect verification lapse to outcome and articulate prevention-focused lessons learned.
initial_messages: Debrief. The critical miss was approving start work without proving zero energy—specifically, the bleeder/vent was never opened.
Lessons learned: what verification dialogue should have happened, and what will you do differently next time before you sign off?

tasks.description: Learner names the missed verification step and prevention behaviors (evidence-based SWC + zero energy proof, closed-loop comms, stop-work authority).
tasks.completion_criteria: Learner identifies the missed bleeder/vent verification and states at least 2 concrete prevention behaviors.

evaluation_criteria.name: Communication
evaluation_criteria.description: Effective communication with control room and crew using three-way (call-out, repeat-back, confirm) communication and clarifying questions.
criteria: Learner asks clarifying questions instead of jumping to conclusions when a procedure isn’t followed.
Learner uses three-way communication (call-out, repeat-back, confirm).
Learner confirms crew awareness of scope and critical steps before authorizing start.

evaluation_criteria.name: Safeguard Verification
evaluation_criteria.description: Learner verifies critical safeguards using evidence-based dialogue and uses stop-work authority when safeguards cannot be verified.
criteria: Learner verifies isolations and safeguards using the isolation list.
Learner verifies SWC through dialogue focused on top critical safeguards (not checkbox acceptance).
Learner challenges reliance on gauge/control-room-only confirmation where proof is required.
Learner uses stop-work authority when zero energy cannot be proven.
Learner reinforces time/expectation to perform verification as designed.
